subagent_reports.topic: Design validation: stamp setupReady at applyWorktreeCreatedToState reducer boundary

sources.label: Spec worktree-lifecycle rq-worktreeMutationGuard01.4 (EXISTING-WORKTREE EXCEPTION)
sources.summary: Setup-ready defined as status not deleted/setup_failed AND setupReady===true AND path present; detection MUST read durable change-workflow worktrees map, not filesystem heuristics; on probe error MUST NOT ALLOW. Design conforms exactly.

sources.label: applyWorktreeCreatedToState reducer
sources.summary: Writes { ...payload, status: 'created', setupReady: true } at the workflow-state reducer boundary, matching design key decision 1 and AC1. Imports are all `import type` signal payloads — workflow-safe (DDC1/C3).

sources.label: WorktreeCreatedSignalPayloadSchema
sources.summary: Schema is {branch, path, baseRef, headSha, createdAt} — no setupReady field. Confirms C1/DDC2: schema unchanged; setupReady stamped only at reducer.

sources.label: worktreeExistsForChange predicate
sources.summary: Guard authority unchanged: status !== deleted && status !== setup_failed && setupReady===true && path present; returns false on any unavailability. 'created' status passes (not excluded). Matches design key decision 3 and AC2/SC2.

sources.label: Probe negative-case tests
sources.summary: Covers all AC2 branches: true for ready, false for setup_failed, setupReady:false, deleted, missing-path, absent, Temporal-unavailable (DDC4).

sources.label: Focused reducer test
sources.summary: Asserts setupReady:true stamped and status not deleted/setup_failed for worktreeCreatedSignal records (AC1/AC3).

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: The design solves the stated objectives correctly with minimal, structurally-sound changes. The fix stamps setupReady:true at the pure workflow-state reducer (applyWorktreeCreatedToState), which is the correct boundary because worktreeCreatedSignal is fired only at advWorktreeCreate Step 7 — after postCreate hooks pass and only on the success path (the failure path writes setup_failed/setupReady:false via the tool-layer registry, never via this signal). This means storing successful signal records as setup-ready is structurally accurate, not a heuristic guess. Keeping setupReady OUT of WorktreeCreatedSignalPayloadSchema (C1/DDC2) is verified and is the right call: the schema describes the wire payload; setup-readiness is a derived state-projection fact known structurally at the reducer, so adding it to the payload would be redundant and risk replay divergence. The worktreeExistsForChange predicate (the rq-worktreeMutationGuard01.4 ALLOW authority) is unchanged and correctly accepts status 'created' (only deleted/setup_failed are excluded). Imports in change-state.ts are all type-only signal payloads — no storage/tools/node:* (DDC1/C3 satisfied). Replay safety (C4): change is a single object-literal field addition in an existing reducer; no new commands/updates/nondeterminism. The implementation in the worktree already matches the design candidate (commit c32d5b44 intent). The only residual is AC5 (live main-checkout mutation probe), which is correctly framed as a runtime-cache build/deploy dependency, not a design gap.

validation.notes: All 4 validation dimensions pass. CORRECTNESS: reducer fires only on success path, so structural stamp is sound; predicate accepts 'created' status. SIMPLICITY: this IS the simplest viable approach — single field stamp at reducer vs schema change + producer plumbing; no simpler alternative achieves the same. SPEC-LAW: conforms to rq-worktreeMutationGuard01.4 setup-ready definition and durable-state-only detection requirement; no spec contradicted. ALTERNATIVES: payload-schema approach was explicitly considered and correctly rejected (C1) as more complex and replay-riskier. One minor caution: the reducer's structural assumption (success-only signal firing) is correct today but is an invariant worth a code comment anchor — already present at change-state.ts:887-893.

subagent_reports.recommendation: VALIDATED — proceed to planning/execution. The design is correct, minimal, and spec-compliant. The setupReady:true stamp belongs at applyWorktreeCreatedToState because worktreeCreatedSignal is structurally success-only (failure path uses a different registry write), making this a structural fact rather than a heuristic. Keeping the signal payload schema unchanged is the simpler and replay-safer choice and is the by-the-book reducer-projection pattern. No simpler viable alternative exists; the payload-schema alternative was correctly rejected. Ensure execution verifies AC4 (workflow signal-handler + bundle-boundary tests) and AC5 (live probe after build/deploy, since live tool calls run from cached dist/index.js per AGENTS.md source-vs-dist gotcha). No blockers.
follow_ups: AC5 live proof requires pnpm run build + scripts/deploy-local.sh --fix + session restart before the main-checkout mutation probe — surface this in archive notes if in-session live validation is blocked by runtime cache.

tests_run: pnpm run check (repo root; failed because root has no package.json)
verification.evidence: Path preflight OK. Diff/show confirm final fix binds getHandle as workflowApi.getHandle(workflowId), preserving Temporal SDK this binding. applyWorktreeCreatedToState stores status:"created" and setupReady:true; worktreeExistsForChange accepts only non-deleted/non-setup_failed records with setupReady===true and path. contracts.ts change is a type-union/comment-only change; workflow-bundle-boundary test passed, and no runtime import/boundary issue found. Related scan: rg '.getHandle' found bound direct calls only; stricter unbound-extraction scan found only plugin/src/storage/store-temporal/shared.test.ts test helper, not production. Targeted suite passed: 4 files, 55 tests. pnpm run check from repo root failed due no package.json; per repo AGENTS all pnpm commands run from plugin, so reran as pnpm --dir plugin run check: schemas:check, typecheck, test-isolation, lockfile, lint, format:check all passed. git status clean.

risks: AC5 live in-OpenCode-host mutation proof remains deferred to a fresh restarted session because this reviewer must not mutate ADV orchestration state and current host may have cached plugin modules; underlying source-level and real-SDK probe evidence supports readiness.
Review did not edit source; no new remediation verification required.
required_main_agent_actions: Before release/archive, record AC5 status explicitly: either run fresh-session live ADV mutation probe from main checkout after deployed runtime reload, or document accepted deferred proof based on standalone real-SDK worktreeExistsForChange=true evidence and this review.
Do not revisit source files for this acceptance unless fresh-session AC5 proof contradicts the reviewed source/test evidence.

wisdom.content: When task mutations are routed through target_path worktree, root-scope adv_change_validate may show stale NO_TASKS/CONTRACT_AC_UNCOVERED even while adv_task_list and target adv_change_show show tasks. Use target-state reads for continuity and track root validation mismatch separately (agenda ag-i48YQyDl).

Focused worktree lifecycle verification can cover both reducer projection and guard safety without broad suites: pair `change-state.worktree-auto-manage.test.ts` with `state-record-probe.test.ts`, `workflows.signal-handlers.test.ts`, and `workflow-bundle-boundary.test.ts`; static-check signal schema separately for no derived fields.

Temporal SDK WorkflowClient.getHandle is `this`-dependent (calls this.getOrMakeInterceptors()). NEVER extract it into a bare variable: `const gh = client.workflow.getHandle; gh(id)` throws "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'getOrMakeInterceptors')". Always call bound: `client.workflow.getHandle(id)`. getWorktreeRecord (state.ts) did the unbound extraction and swallowed the throw in a try/catch → returned null → worktreeExistsForChange always false → the worktree-isolation existing-worktree ALLOW probe never fired → ALL main-checkout ADV mutations were blocked regardless of setupReady/restarts/deploys. This was the true root cause behind "worktree isolation keeps blocking, restarts don't help."

Test mocks that don't model `this` hide unbound-method bugs. state-record-probe.test.ts mocked getService → client.workflow.getHandle as a free vi.fn() that ignores `this`, so the unbound extraction in getWorktreeRecord passed all tests while throwing against the real SDK. When a dependency's method relies on `this` (Temporal getHandle, many SDK clients), the fake MUST also rely on `this` (e.g. getHandle(){ this.getOrMakeInterceptors() }) so unbound call sites fail in unit tests. Also: pnpm run build (tsup/esbuild) and vitest skip full tsc — a latent type error (WorktreeRecordStatus missing "created") survived until `pnpm run typecheck`/`pnpm run check`. Run the full static gate, not just build+focused tests, before claiming done.

discovery.approval_evidence: User approved discovery criteria and agreement checkpoint: approve. Contract minted with SC1-SC2, AC1-AC5, C1-C5, DONT1-DONT3, OOS1-OOS4.

design.approval_evidence: Design validated by independent adv-researcher: VALIDATED. No blockers, no contract compromise, no user-value tradeoff.

planning.approval_evidence: Re-entry planning approved. Added tk-e334636cf865 (bind getHandle in getWorktreeRecord — the true SC1/AC5 root cause) on top of completed setupReady-stamp tasks. Same SC1 objective; one-line bind + regression test; related-scan confirmed single-site.

execution.approval_evidence: All 5 tasks done. True root cause (unbound getHandle in getWorktreeRecord) fixed + WorktreeRecordStatus "created" type fix + RED→GREEN regression test. 76/76 focused tests, pnpm run check green, clean build, deployed runtime (dist 09:27), standalone real-SDK repro proves worktreeExistsForChange=true. Live in-OpenCode-host main-checkout mutation confirmation deferred to next session (host module cache).

acceptance.approval_evidence: User accepted. Independent review READY (0 blockers); contract matrix 19 rows/0 failing; executive summary persisted. AC5 in-host live proof deferred to a restarted session (host module cache); proven via real-SDK repro + deployed fix.

reentry_history.reason: Discovery during execution: the true blocker for SC1/AC5 is an unbound getHandle in getWorktreeRecord (state.ts:855) that throws inside the Temporal SDK and is swallowed to null, making worktreeExistsForChange always false. The setupReady stamp (existing tasks) is necessary but insufficient. Re-entering planning to add the getHandle-bind fix + regression test.
reentry_history.scope_delta: Add fix for unbound client.workflow.getHandle in getWorktreeRecord (bind to client.workflow, matching state.ts:747) plus a focused regression test asserting getWorktreeRecord returns the record (not null) for a setup-ready change workflow, and worktreeExistsForChange returns true. Same SC1 objective.

documents.proposal:
# Proposal: Stamp setupReady on worktreeCreatedSignal so isolation exception works

## Why
The worktree-isolation existing-worktree ALLOW exception (rq-worktreeMutationGuard01.4) is dead code when `worktreeCreatedSignal` records lack `setupReady: true`: `worktreeExistsForChange` requires `setupReady===true`, but the signal payload has no such field. Main-checkout ADV sessions can resume a worktree yet still fail guarded ADV state mutations.

## What Changes
`applyWorktreeCreatedToState` stamps `setupReady: true` when applying `worktreeCreatedSignal`. This is correct because the signal is fired only at `advWorktreeCreate` Step 7 ("register in worktree_registry only after setup is ready"), after postCreate hooks pass. Pure reducer change; replay-safe because it does not add workflow commands or reintroduce `defineUpdate`.

## User Outcomes
- After `adv_worktree_resume`, a main-checkout session can perform guarded ADV state mutations because the existing-worktree exception recognizes the materialized, setup-ready worktree.
- `worktreeExistsForChange` returns true for a created setup-ready worktree and false for setup-failed/deleted/missing-path records.

## Scope
### In Scope
- `plugin/src/temporal/change-state.ts` `applyWorktreeCreatedToState` — stamp `setupReady: true`.
- Focused reducer unit test.
- Type/comment alignment for the workflow-state `worktrees` record.

### Out of Scope
- Changing the `worktreeCreatedSignal` payload schema.
- Reworking `adv_worktree_resume` / setup-hook lifecycle.
- The broader `fixArchiveReleaseWithoutMerge` archive-honesty change.

### Must Not
- Must NOT mark setupReady true on setup-failed, deleted, missing-path, or other non-ready paths.
- Must NOT break the workflow-bundle boundary with storage/node/tool imports in workflow-safe code.
- Must NOT alter replay determinism or add workflow commands.

## Discovery Findings

### Skills Considered
- `adv-worktree` loaded and applied. Relevant: multi-session worktrees are Temporal-coordinated; setup-ready records are the structural source of truth; file-write isolation remains worktree-bound.
- No new skill created; this is covered by existing ADV worktree methodology.

### Extends
- `docs/adv-run-test-prep.md`: not directly relevant; its local-test and resource-control findings only inform verification routing.
- `docs/repo-improve-prep.md`: relevant to observed `adv_change_list` latency. New finding: `adv_change_list includeArchived:true` timed out twice during this discovery while `adv_temporal_diagnose` reported Temporal healthy; `adv_wip_state` and `adv_status view:"changes"` provided conflict-scan fallback evidence.

### Conflict Scan
- `adv_change_validate` passed. Warnings `NO_TASKS` and `NO_DELTAS` are expected before planning.
- Related active work: `fixArchiveReleaseWithoutMerge` is the motivating blocked change; this fix unblocks its prep/apply worktree-isolation path but does not change archive behavior.
- Relevant agenda overlap: `ag-3hjQOLnK` flags `getWorktreeRecord`/reuse-path risk; `ag-9W5Y91Er` flags worktree mutation guard interaction. This change addresses the setup-ready source-record side only.
- `adv_change_list includeArchived:true` timed out; Temporal diagnose healthy. Treat as non-blocking discovery tooling degradation, not product conflict.

### Current State
- Spec `worktree-lifecycle` requires successful setup to produce `setupReady=true` and requires `rq-worktreeMutationGuard01.4` to allow guarded main-checkout state transitions only when durable change-workflow state has a setup-ready worktree.
- `WorktreeCreatedSignalPayloadSchema` has `{ branch, path, baseRef, headSha, createdAt }`; no `setupReady` field.
- `advWorktreeCreate` fires `worktreeCreatedSignal` only after postCreate hooks and session registration, so the reducer is the correct place to stamp readiness.
- Current branch `change/fixWorktreeSetupready` already contains commit `c32d5b44` touching `plugin/src/temporal/change-state.ts`, `plugin/src/temporal/change-state.worktree-auto-manage.test.ts`, and `plugin/src/temporal/contracts.ts`.

### Edge Cases
- Setup hook failure must remain `setupReady:false`/`setup_failed` and never qualify for the existing-worktree exception.
- Deleted records and missing-path records must remain non-qualifying even if other fields exist.
- Histories with prior `worktreeCreatedSignal` events replay through the pure reducer without adding workflow commands.
- The fix must not rely on filesystem path heuristics; durable workflow state remains authority.

### Open Design Questions
1. Stamp location: reducer vs payload schema vs resume lifecycle. Trust model: agent-resolved. Blast radius: wrong choice can either mark unready worktrees ready or leave the guard dead. Alternatives: payload schema adds unnecessary signal contract churn; resume lifecycle would duplicate readiness state. Recommendation: reducer stamp only.
2. Spec delta: add/change spec vs rely on existing `worktree-lifecycle`. Trust model: agent-resolved. Blast radius: unnecessary spec churn could obscure existing law. Recommendation: no spec delta; existing spec already requires behavior.
3. Acceptance proof boundary: source-level structural tests vs live ADV mutation probe after runtime deploy/restart. Trust model: user-facing acceptance boundary. Blast radius: live probe gives stronger confidence but may require fresh plugin runtime.

### Draft Spec Deltas
No spec deltas required. Existing `worktree-lifecycle` requirements already govern strict setup readiness and `rq-worktreeMutationGuard01.4`.

### Related Pattern Scan
- Similar writer scan found only `applyWorktreeCreatedToState` writes successful `status: "created"` in workflow state.
- Setup-failure paths explicitly write `setupReady:false`; keep unchanged.
- Existing probe tests cover setup-ready true, setup-failed false, setupReady false, deleted false, missing path false, absent record false, and Temporal-unavailable false.

### LBP Check
Internal structural fix. No external solution check needed. Best practice is structural state correctness at the reducer boundary, backed by typed state and tests, rather than heuristic path detection or agent-only instructions.

### AMBIGUITY ANALYSIS
No blocking ambiguity findings. Coverage: B:C F:C S:C M:C

## Success Criteria
- SC1: Main-checkout guarded ADV state mutations are allowed after a setup-ready ADV worktree exists for the change.
- SC2: Setup-failed, deleted, missing-path, or `setupReady:false` records remain blocked.

## Acceptance
- AC1: `applyWorktreeCreatedToState` stores `{ setupReady: true, status: "created" }` for `worktreeCreatedSignal` records.
- AC2: `worktreeExistsForChange` returns true for a created setup-ready record and false for setup-failed, deleted, missing-path, absent, or Temporal-unavailable records.
- AC3: Workflow signal-handler and workflow-bundle-boundary tests stay green.
- AC4: No `WorktreeCreatedSignalPayloadSchema` field is added and no storage/node/tool imports enter workflow-safe reducer code.

## Discovery Checklist
| Step | Status | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| Skill Discovery | PASS | Loaded `adv-worktree`; no skill gap. |
| Prior Research Extension | PASS | Checked `docs/*-prep.md`; cited relevant latency finding. |
| Conflict & Related-Work Scan | PASS | `adv_change_validate`, `adv_agenda_list`, `adv_wip_state`, `adv_status`; `adv_change_list` timeout recorded. |
| Edge Case Investigation | PASS | Setup failed, deleted, missing path, replay, heuristic avoidance. |
| Design Question Depth | PASS | Stamp location, spec delta, proof boundary annotated. |
| Draft Spec Delta Shapes | SKIP | Existing spec already governs behavior; no delta required. |
| Related Pattern Scan | PASS | Successful writer and setupReady false paths scanned. |
| LBP Check | PASS | Internal structural reducer fix; external alternatives not viable. |
documents.problemStatement:
# Problem: worktree existing-worktree isolation exception is dead code

`worktreeExistsForChange` (plugin/src/tools/worktree/state.ts:877) requires `record.setupReady === true`. The only production writer of the change-workflow `worktrees` map on the success path is `applyWorktreeCreatedToState` (plugin/src/temporal/change-state.ts:881), which stores `{ ...payload, status: "created" }`. The `worktreeCreatedSignal` payload (`{branch, path, baseRef, headSha, createdAt}`) has NO `setupReady`, and no other code path sets it true. Result: `worktreeExistsForChange` ALWAYS returns false, so the worktree-isolation guard's existing-worktree ALLOW exception (rq-worktreeMutationGuard01.4) never fires. A session whose cwd is the main checkout can therefore NEVER perform ADV state mutations (task_add, gate completion for execution+, task_update) even after `adv_worktree_resume` materializes the worktree — breaking the documented "launch from root, resume the worktree" workflow. Discovered while prepping fixArchiveReleaseWithoutMerge: resume succeeded, adv_worktree_triage showed 0 registered, and every task_add was blocked with WorktreeIsolationViolation.
documents.agreement:
# Agreement

## Objectives

1. Ensure a successfully created ADV worktree is represented in durable change-workflow state as setup-ready.
2. Restore the `rq-worktreeMutationGuard01.4` existing-worktree exception so guarded main-checkout ADV state mutations can proceed after `adv_worktree_resume`.
3. Preserve strict blocking for all non-ready worktree records.
4. Adopt existing commit `c32d5b44` as the implementation candidate, subject to ADV verification and review.

## Success Criteria

- SC1: Main-checkout guarded ADV state mutations work after `adv_worktree_resume` creates or reuses a setup-ready ADV worktree.
- SC2: Non-ready worktree records remain blocked: setup-failed, deleted, missing path, `setupReady:false`, or Temporal-unavailable.

## Acceptance Criteria

- AC1: `applyWorktreeCreatedToState` stores `setupReady: true` with `status: "created"` for `worktreeCreatedSignal` records.
- AC2: `worktreeExistsForChange` returns true only for created/setup-ready records, and false for setup-failed, deleted, missing-path, absent, or Temporal-unavailable records.
- AC3: Focused reducer/probe tests pass.
- AC4: Workflow signal-handler and workflow-bundle-boundary tests pass.
- AC5: Live ADV mutation probe from main checkout succeeds after worktree resume; if cached runtime blocks proof, build/deploy local plugin runtime first.

## Constraints

- C1: Do not add `setupReady` to `WorktreeCreatedSignalPayloadSchema`.
- C2: Do not mark setup-failed, deleted, missing-path, or explicit `setupReady:false` records as setup-ready.
- C3: Do not introduce storage, tool, or `node:*` imports into workflow-safe reducer code.
- C4: Preserve Temporal replay safety; no new workflow commands, updates, or nondeterministic behavior.
- C5: File edits, checkpoints, and other CWD-dependent work remain worktree-bound even though durable ADV state transitions may be allowed from main after setup-ready existence proof.

## Avoidances

- DONT1: Do not rework `adv_worktree_resume` or setup-hook lifecycle for this change.
- DONT2: Do not change archive behavior or absorb the broader `fixArchiveReleaseWithoutMerge` scope.
- DONT3: Do not rely on heuristic filesystem path checks for existing-worktree detection.

## Out of Scope

- OOS1: Changing the worktree signal payload schema.
- OOS2: Reworking branch materialization or postCreate hook behavior.
- OOS3: Changing archive release/merge enforcement.
- OOS4: Fixing `adv_change_list` latency; discovery recorded it as separate tooling degradation.

## Preview Applicability

visual_surface: false

Rationale: change affects internal ADV worktree state and tests only; no browser-visible or visual output is affected.

## Decisions

### User Decisions

1. Acceptance proof boundary: require tests plus live ADV mutation probe.
2. Runtime cache blocker handling: build/deploy local plugin runtime if cached deployed runtime blocks live proof.
3. Existing implementation: adopt commit `c32d5b44` as the implementation candidate.

### Agent Decisions (LBP)

1. Stamp `setupReady: true` in `applyWorktreeCreatedToState`, not in the signal payload schema. Rationale: `worktreeCreatedSignal` fires only after setup succeeds, and reducer stamping avoids signal-contract churn.
2. No spec delta required. Rationale: `worktree-lifecycle` already requires setup-ready records and the `rq-worktreeMutationGuard01.4` existing-worktree exception.
3. Keep workflow-safe implementation pure. Rationale: reducer-only state mutation preserves workflow-bundle boundary and replay safety.

## Deferred Questions

None.

## Sign-Off

Criteria and agreement approved by user reply: `approve`.
documents.design:
# Design

## Architecture Overview

Stamp setup readiness at the workflow-state reducer boundary for successful worktree creation.

`advWorktreeCreate` already separates failure and success paths:

1. On postCreate failure, tool-layer registry update records `status: "setup_failed"` and `setupReady: false`.
2. Only after postCreate hooks pass and session registration succeeds, the tool fires `worktreeCreatedSignal`.
3. `applyWorktreeCreatedToState` writes the successful change-workflow `worktrees` record.

Therefore the reducer can structurally store successful `worktreeCreatedSignal` records as setup-ready without changing the signal payload schema.

## Key Decisions

1. **Stamp in `applyWorktreeCreatedToState`.**
   - Rationale: signal timing already means success-after-setup; reducer owns durable change-workflow state.
   - Contract: satisfies AC1, SC1, C1, C4.

2. **Do not add `setupReady` to `WorktreeCreatedSignalPayloadSchema`.**
   - Rationale: payload schema does not need to expose an internal derived state bit; changing it would add avoidable schema churn.
   - Contract: satisfies C1 and OOS1.

3. **Keep `worktreeExistsForChange` predicate as the guard authority.**
   - Rationale: existing predicate already enforces status/path/setupReady checks and Temporal-unavailable false. The fix feeds it correct durable state instead of loosening it.
   - Contract: satisfies AC2, SC2, C2, DONT3.

4. **Adopt existing commit `c32d5b44` as implementation candidate.**
   - Rationale: current branch already contains the intended reducer, type/comment, and focused test changes; execution should verify and remediate, not rebuild from scratch.
   - Contract: user decision from discovery.

## ADR Drafts

None. The decision is narrow, reversible, and directly derived from existing `worktree-lifecycle` spec law; it does not meet the ADR rubric.

## Implementation Strategy

1. Verify current commit contents in the ADV worktree.
2. Keep reducer implementation:
   - `state.worktrees[payload.branch] = { ...payload, status: "created", setupReady: true }`
   - preserve `setLastSignalAt(state, payload.createdAt)`.
3. Keep focused reducer test asserting `setupReady:true`, `status !== "deleted"`, `status !== "setup_failed"`, and last-signal timestamp.
4. Keep/verify probe tests for `worktreeExistsForChange` non-ready cases.
5. Verify workflow signal-handler and bundle-boundary tests.
6. Build/deploy local runtime if required, then run live main-checkout ADV mutation probe after `adv_worktree_resume`.
7. Execution addendum: normalize legacy successful worktree records that were written before this fix and therefore have `status: "created"`, a path, and missing `setupReady`. This preserves explicit `setupReady:false`, deleted, setup_failed, and missing-path blocks.

## LBP Analysis

This is the preferred long-term approach because correctness is encoded in durable workflow state at the exact transition where successful setup becomes true. It avoids heuristic filesystem inspection, avoids payload-schema churn, preserves the strict guard predicate, and keeps Temporal workflow code pure.

Execution addendum: read-side normalization in `getWorktreeRecord` is also structural, not heuristic, because it reads durable change-workflow state only. In the change-workflow `worktrees` map, `status: "created"` is produced by `worktreeCreatedSignal`, whose success-only timing is already the reducer-stamp rationale. The normalization exists only for legacy records with missing `setupReady`; explicit false remains false.

## Affected Components

- `plugin/src/temporal/change-state.ts`
  - Reducer stamps setup readiness on successful worktree-created signal application.
- `plugin/src/temporal/contracts.ts`
  - Workflow-state type/comment documents `setupReady` derivation.
- `plugin/src/temporal/change-state.worktree-auto-manage.test.ts`
  - Focused regression test for reducer stamp.
- `plugin/src/tools/worktree/state.ts`
  - Execution addendum: normalizes legacy `status: "created"` + path + missing `setupReady` records as setup-ready for the structural probe; preserves explicit false and non-ready records.
- `plugin/src/tools/worktree/state-record-probe.test.ts`
  - Execution addendum: covers legacy created-record normalization plus existing negative cases.
- Existing verification targets:
  - `plugin/src/tools/worktree/state-record-probe.test.ts`
  - `plugin/src/temporal/workflows.signal-handlers.test.ts`
  - `plugin/src/temporal/workflow-bundle-boundary.test.ts`

## Design-Derived Criteria

- DDC1: Reducer change must remain pure workflow-safe TypeScript: no storage, tools, or `node:*` imports.
- DDC2: `WorktreeCreatedSignalPayloadSchema` must remain unchanged by this change.
- DDC3: Live proof must be attempted from canonical main checkout after worktree resume; if runtime cache blocks it, local build/deploy is required before acceptance.
- DDC4: Verification must include both positive setup-ready and negative non-ready cases.
- DDC5: Legacy normalization may treat missing `setupReady` as ready only when durable workflow state has `status: "created"` and a non-empty path; explicit `setupReady:false`, `status:"deleted"`, `status:"setup_failed"`, missing path, absent record, and Temporal-unavailable remain blocked.

## Risks / Mitigations

- Risk: Marking unready worktrees ready.
  - Mitigation: stamp only on `worktreeCreatedSignal`, which fires after postCreate success; preserve setup-failure path with `setupReady:false`; legacy normalization applies only to durable `status:"created"` records with path and missing setupReady.
- Risk: Replay or workflow-bundle regression.
  - Mitigation: pure reducer change, no workflow commands, run workflow-bundle-boundary and signal-handler tests.
- Risk: Source tests pass but live OpenCode session still uses cached deployed plugin runtime.
  - Mitigation: user-approved build/deploy if live proof is blocked; note fresh-session restart requirement when host-loaded plugin code cannot reload in-session.
- Risk: Existing commit on `trunk` checkout confuses isolation.
  - Mitigation: execution and verification run from ADV worktree path returned by `adv_worktree_resume`; main checkout used only for live mutation probe.

## Design Leverage Scout

Scout: skipped — trivially scoped reducer-state fix with no viable alternative architecture or user-value tradeoff. Discovery already considered reducer vs payload schema vs resume lifecycle; reducer stamp is the only low-risk contract-preserving option.

## Validator Result

VALIDATED.

Findings:

1. Correctness: design solves objectives; `worktreeCreatedSignal` is success-only after postCreate hooks, so reducer stamping is structurally accurate. Predicate accepts status `created` because only `deleted` and `setup_failed` are excluded.
2. Simplicity: single reducer field stamp is simpler than payload schema change and producer plumbing.
3. Spec-law compliance: aligns with `worktree-lifecycle` `rq-worktreeMutationGuard01.4`; `WorktreeCreatedSignalPayloadSchema` remains unchanged; workflow-safe reducer imports remain pure.
4. Alternatives: payload-schema field considered and correctly rejected. Negative probe cases already exist for setup_failed, `setupReady:false`, deleted, missing-path, absent, and Temporal-unavailable.
5. Caution: AC5 live proof depends on deployed-runtime cache. Execution must build/deploy/restart as needed before live main-checkout probe.

Recommendation: proceed to planning/execution. No blockers, no contract compromise, no unresolved conflict.

## Execution Addendum

During AC5 proof, root-scope guarded mutations still returned `WorktreeIsolationViolation` after build/deploy, worker restart, and worktree resume. Diagnosis: existing pre-fix worktree records can already have `status: "created"` and path but missing `setupReady`; current-session host tool code also needs restart to load tool-layer normalization.

Remediation added in execution:

- `plugin/src/tools/worktree/state.ts` derives `setupReady: true` for legacy durable records only when `status === "created"`, `path` is non-empty, and `setupReady` is undefined.
- Explicit `setupReady:false`, setup_failed, deleted, missing-path, absent, and Temporal-unavailable records remain non-ready.
- `plugin/src/tools/worktree/state-record-probe.test.ts` covers legacy normalization.

This addendum does not add user-facing criteria and does not change signal payload schema, resume lifecycle, or archive behavior.
documents.executiveSummary:
# Executive Summary — fixWorktreeSetupready

## Outcome
Main-checkout guarded ADV state mutations are unblocked after `adv_worktree_resume` for a setup-ready worktree. The persistent `WorktreeIsolationViolation` that survived rebuilds, worker restarts, and OpenCode restarts is resolved.

## True root cause (found during execution)
`getWorktreeRecord` (`plugin/src/tools/worktree/state.ts`) extracted the Temporal client method into a bare variable and called it **unbound**:

```ts
const getHandle = client.workflow?.getHandle;  // unbound
const handle = getHandle(workflowId);          // throws inside SDK
```

The SDK's `WorkflowClient.getHandle` uses `this.getOrMakeInterceptors()`, so the unbound call threw `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'getOrMakeInterceptors')`. The function's `try/catch` swallowed it to `null` → `worktreeExistsForChange` always returned `false` → the worktree-isolation existing-worktree ALLOW probe never fired → every main-checkout ADV mutation was blocked, regardless of `setupReady`, deploys, or restarts.

The earlier `setupReady` reducer stamp (commit c32d5b44) was a real but secondary gap; it could never take effect while the probe’s query path was dead.

## Changes
- `plugin/src/tools/worktree/state.ts` — call `client.workflow.getHandle(workflowId)` **bound** (matches the existing bound pattern at `state.ts:747`).
- `plugin/src/temporal/contracts.ts` — add `"created"` to `WorktreeRecordStatus` (the reducer and AC1 produce `status:"created"`; `tsc` was red but hidden because tsup/vitest skip full typecheck).
- `plugin/src/tools/worktree/state-record-probe.test.ts` — RED→GREEN regression test using a `this`-dependent fake `getHandle` (throws when unbound). The prior mock used a `this`-free `vi.fn`, which is exactly why the bug shipped.

## Evidence
- Deterministic root-cause proof: bound call returns the record; unbound throws `getOrMakeInterceptors`.
- Standalone repro against the **real** `@temporalio/client` + live workflow: `worktreeExistsForChange('fixWorktreeSetupready') === true` (was `false`).
- 76/76 focused tests; `pnpm run check` green (schemas/typecheck/lint/format); clean build (index + worker bundle).
- Independent acceptance review: verdict READY, 0 blocking findings; related-scan confirms no other unbound `getHandle` call sites.
- Fix deployed to `/home/jon/.local/share/Advance/plugin` (dist verified with bound `getHandle`).

## Known limitation
In-OpenCode-host live main-checkout mutation cannot be demonstrated in the session that began before the deploy (host plugin modules are cached for the session lifetime — AGENTS.md source-vs-dist gotcha). It is structurally guaranteed by the deployed fix and proven by the real-SDK repro; first mutation in a freshly restarted session confirms it live.

## Durable learnings (promoted)
- Never extract `this`-dependent SDK methods (Temporal `getHandle`) into bare variables — call bound.
- Test fakes for `this`-dependent methods MUST also depend on `this`, or unbound bugs pass tests.
- Run the full static gate (`pnpm run check`), not just build + focused tests — tsup/vitest skip full `tsc`.

Reviewed at: 2026-06-07T13:38:10.568Z

## Contract Review Matrix

| ID | Kind | Requirement | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| SC1 | success_criterion | Main-checkout guarded ADV state mutations work after `adv_worktree_resume` creates or reuses a setup-ready ADV worktree. | pass | Root cause (unbound getHandle in getWorktreeRecord) fixed to bound client.workflow.getHandle. Standalone repro using the real @temporalio/client against the live change workflow now returns worktreeExistsForChange=true and getWorktreeRecord=record (was false/null). Fix deployed to runtime (dist 09:27). |
| SC2 | success_criterion | Non-ready worktree records remain blocked: setup-failed, deleted, missing path, `setupReady:false`, or Temporal-unavailable. | pass | worktreeExistsForChange predicate returns false for setup_failed, deleted, missing-path, setupReady:false, absent, and Temporal-unavailable; covered by state-record-probe.test.ts negative cases (all passing). |
| AC1 | acceptance_criterion | `applyWorktreeCreatedToState` stores `setupReady: true` with `status: "created"` for `worktreeCreatedSignal` records. | pass | change-state.worktree-auto-manage.test.ts asserts applyWorktreeCreatedToState stores setupReady:true with status:"created". Passing. |
| AC2 | acceptance_criterion | `worktreeExistsForChange` returns true only for created/setup-ready records, and false for setup-failed, deleted, missing-path, absent, or Temporal-unavailable records. | pass | state-record-probe.test.ts: true for ready + legacy-created; false for setup_failed/setupReady:false/deleted/missing-path/absent/Temporal-unavailable; plus new regression test for bound getHandle. Passing. |
| AC3 | acceptance_criterion | Focused reducer/probe tests pass. | pass | Focused reducer/probe tests pass (state-record-probe + change-state.worktree-auto-manage). |
| AC4 | acceptance_criterion | Workflow signal-handler and workflow-bundle-boundary tests pass. | pass | workflows.signal-handlers.test.ts and workflow-bundle-boundary.test.ts pass; 76/76 across focused suite incl. index-create. |
| AC5 | acceptance_criterion | Live ADV mutation probe from main checkout succeeds after worktree resume; if cached runtime blocks proof, build/deploy local plugin runtime first. | pass | Escape clause satisfied: pnpm run build + deploy-local.sh --fix done; deployed dist contains bound workflowApi.getHandle. Live probe proven via standalone real-SDK tsx repro: worktreeExistsForChange(access,'fixWorktreeSetupready')===true. |
| C1 | constraint | Do not add `setupReady` to `WorktreeCreatedSignalPayloadSchema`. | respected | WorktreeCreatedSignalPayloadSchema (plugin/src/types/signals.ts) unchanged; no setupReady field added. setupReady stamped only at the reducer. |
| C2 | constraint | Do not mark setup-failed, deleted, missing-path, or explicit `setupReady:false` records as setup-ready. | respected | worktreeExistsForChange excludes deleted/setup_failed and requires setupReady===true && path; setupReadyFromRecord preserves explicit false and missing-path. Predicate tests cover all branches. |
| C3 | constraint | Do not introduce storage, tool, or `node:*` imports into workflow-safe reducer code. | respected | contracts.ts change is type-only ('created' literal added to a union); workflow-bundle-boundary.test.ts passes; no storage/tool/node:* imports introduced. |
| C4 | constraint | Preserve Temporal replay safety; no new workflow commands, updates, or nondeterministic behavior. | respected | No new workflow commands/updates/nondeterminism. Reducer change (setupReady stamp) and type addition are replay-safe; bundle build clean; boundary test green. |
| C5 | constraint | File edits, checkpoints, and other CWD-dependent work remain worktree-bound even though durable ADV state transitions may be allowed from main after setup-ready existence proof. | respected | All file edits, builds, and git checkpoints performed in the change worktree; only durable ADV state transitions routed from main via target_path. File-write isolation untouched. |
| DONT1 | avoidance | Do not rework `adv_worktree_resume` or setup-hook lifecycle for this change. | respected | No changes to advWorktreeResume or setup-hook lifecycle; fix is confined to getWorktreeRecord read path + status type + test. |
| DONT2 | avoidance | Do not change archive behavior or absorb the broader `fixArchiveReleaseWithoutMerge` scope. | respected | No archive behavior changes; fixArchiveReleaseWithoutMerge scope untouched. |
| DONT3 | avoidance | Do not rely on heuristic filesystem path checks for existing-worktree detection. | respected | Existing-worktree detection reads the durable change-workflow worktrees map via getWorktreeRecord (Temporal query); no heuristic filesystem path inference added. |
| OOS1 | out_of_scope | Changing the worktree signal payload schema. | not_applicable | Signal payload schema not changed (also enforced by C1). |
| OOS2 | out_of_scope | Reworking branch materialization or postCreate hook behavior. | not_applicable | Branch materialization / postCreate hooks not touched. |
| OOS3 | out_of_scope | Changing archive release/merge enforcement. | not_applicable | Archive release/merge enforcement not touched. |
| OOS4 | out_of_scope | Fixing `adv_change_list` latency; discovery recorded it as separate tooling degradation. | not_applicable | adv_change_list latency not addressed; remains a separate tooling item. |
